title: Success Criteria, Baselines, and Prompt Evals · Mastery Check
summary: Define and test a support-triage prompt before optimizing its wording. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a versioned prompt evaluation harness. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define the user, task, decision, acceptable output, and material failure modes before drafting a prompt.
Turn subjective quality goals into observable criteria and representative test cases.
Create a minimal baseline prompt before adding scaffolding or examples.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Use a changed quiz case for prompt success evals that tests this competency: Combine deterministic checks, rubric review, and human inspection without treating one judge as truth.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for combine deterministic checks, rubric review, and human inspection without treating one judge as truth.

stimulus.title: Judge Review failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Combine deterministic checks, rubric review, and human inspection without treating one judge as truth.

options.text: For diagnose the hidden failure, apply Judge Review without its stated preconditions, omit the a versioned prompt evaluation harness trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Judge Review for a versioned prompt evaluation harness.
Run the Judge Review boundary case for a versioned prompt evaluation harness, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.
Accept the Judge Review result because visible a versioned prompt evaluation harness output is the highest authority. This does not resolve diagnose the hidden failure.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Task Contract evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Task Contract assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Judge Review: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Use a changed quiz case for prompt success evals that tests this competency: Combine deterministic checks, rubric review, and human inspection without treating one judge as truth.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
steps.why: Retrieving Judge Review through the concrete a versioned prompt evaluation harness case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Combine deterministic checks, rubric review, and human inspection without treating one judge as truth.
